description: Make the Agent Workspace chat fully functional like ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini: send images to a vision LLM, show image previews inline, and allow removing attached files.

userStories.title: Upgrade LLM client to support vision (multimodal) messages
userStories.description: As a user, when I attach an image and send a message, the image should actually be read by the AI model.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/lib/llm.ts`, change the function signature to accept messages that can contain image_url content parts (OpenAI vision format)
Switch to a free vision-capable model on OpenRouter. Use `google/gemini-2.0-flash-exp:free` as the primary model (it supports vision)
When a message has attached files that are images, convert them to base64 data URLs and include them as OpenAI-format `image_url` content parts in the user message sent to the API
Keep the streaming SSE parser unchanged
TypeScript must compile with no errors
userStories.notes: The OpenAI vision message format for a user turn with an image is: { role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'text', text: '...' }, { type: 'image_url', image_url: { url: 'data:image/jpeg;base64,...' } }] }

userStories.title: Show image previews inline in chat bubbles + allow removing files
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see attached images rendered inside the chat message I sent, and I want a clear X button to remove files before sending.
acceptanceCriteria: 1. In the attached files preview area (below the input bar), show a thumbnail of the image if the file is an image type, alongside an × remove button
2. When a message is sent with attached images, display them as thumbnails inside the user chat bubble, above the text
3. Store the message content as both the text string AND the file object references so they can be rendered in the history
4. Clear the attached files state after each send
TypeScript must compile with no errors
userStories.notes: Use URL.createObjectURL(file) for the thumbnail preview in the input bar. Use FileReader to convert to base64 before sending to API.
